changes.title: Tandem garage → Bedroom 4 + walk-in robe + ensuite
changes.rationale: Largest single lever: 32.4 m² of room-sized, street-fronting floor area. Moves the property from the 3b/1b comp pool into the 4b/2b pool in one step.

notes: Change of use garage → habitable + loss of covered parking (Hornsby DCP) — DA likely, CDC possible if Housing Code criteria met
Habitable room: ≥2.4 m ceiling, natural light ~10% / ventilation ~5% of floor area — new street-facing window(s)
Slab floor level, damp-proofing and insulation (BASIX) to habitable standard
Ensuite drainage is a new run remote from the east wet stack — hydraulic advice

changes.title: Enclose balcony (7.5 × 2.9 m) → new living area, opened to kitchen/dining
changes.rationale: Banks 21.8 m² under the existing roofline and replaces the living amenity that the Bed 5 conversion takes.

notes: Opening the former external wall between balcony and kitchen/dining is structural — engineer first; pathway likely CDC/DA once engineered
Former balcony floor: waterproofing, floor level and insulation; balustrade edge becomes glazed external wall (BASIX)
Dining/kitchen borrow light through the enclosed zone — re-check light/ventilation ratios
External stair off the balcony is absorbed — retain/relocate yard access and check egress

changes.title: Lounge → Bedroom 5 (hall strip retained on its east side)
changes.rationale: With the enclosed balcony providing living space, the 22.4 m² lounge converts to a large 5th bedroom, jumping into the 5-bed comp band; a 1.0 m hall strip keeps circulation from the entry landing to the living zone.

notes: Non-structural partition + door; existing windows satisfy light/ventilation
Confirm smoke-alarm layout for the extra bedroom (NSW residential requirement)

changes.title: Bed 1 → second master: hall absorbed, ensuite + walk-in robe added
changes.rationale: Owner-directed: central hall folds into the Bed 1 suite (compact landing retained); ensuite + WIR across the south end give the house two master bedrooms — a strong differentiator vs 5-bed comps with 2 baths.

notes: New ensuite drainage at the south end — new run, hydraulic advice
Hall linen storage is lost — flagged as amenity trade-off

changes.title: Bed 2: corner walk-in robe
changes.rationale: Owner-directed: Bed 1 and Bed 2 both get walk-in access; Bed 2 gains a corner WIR.

changes.title: Main bath goes L-shaped with a second WC pocket behind the existing WC
changes.rationale: Owner-directed ('add a WS behind the current WS, bath is L shape' — interpreted as a second WC/shower pocket entered from the bath; flag if wrong). Two WCs + 3 baths suits a 5-bed household.

notes: Within the existing east wet stack — cheapest wet work in the house

changes.title: Kitchen: butler's pantry on the laundry wall, island bench, full cabinetry run; laundry door at the living end for outside access
changes.rationale: Owner-directed: professional-feel kitchen — butler's pantry against the laundry wall, island bench in front of it, cabinetry along that wall; laundry opens off the living end for direct outside access.

notes: Joinery + internal door; laundry external access uses existing external door position
